title: QUIC RFC 9002 Recovery Ledger and Key-Discard Work Item
completion_notes: Optional implementation notes, deviations, or follow-up items.
out_of_scope: RFC 9000 transport framing that does not affect recovery state.
RFC 9001 packet-protection behavior beyond the discard hooks needed for this recovery slice.
summary:
Keep the RFC 9002 loss-detection and key-discard contract visible in the trace system while the runtime-owned sender ledger and selective discard cleanup land.
Preserve the ability to add direct test and benchmark evidence as the recovery code matures.
verification_plan:
Run the targeted RFC 9002 requirement-home tests that exercise the runtime ledger, timer reset, and key-discard paths.
Run the repo-local SpecTrace validation flow so the updated trace links stay honest.
Run the focused QUIC lane test filter and keep the runtime cleanup proof separate from the helper-only secret-discard proof.
planned_changes:
- Keep the per-space sender ledger and packet-protection tagging aligned with the RFC 9002 recovery contract as the implementation lands.
- Keep the Initial and Handshake discard paths tied to PTO reset and bytes-in-flight cleanup.
- Keep the rejected 0-RTT cleanup path tied to the runtime-owned sender ledger rather than helper-only math.
- Keep the trace links stable so the derived trace reports can backfill downstream proof automatically.
